sources_audited.note: IBISWorld page confirms $9.8B 2026 US revenue, 1.89% growth rate, and the industry definition as cited.
Direct fetch returns 403; verified via reader proxy. Confirms 75,300 jobs (2024), $59,440 median wage, 2% growth 2024-34 (slower than average), ~6,900 annual openings mostly from replacement.
Confirms BLS projection downgrade 3% to 2%, the four demand drivers, and the BLS quote that many of these jobs cannot yet be entirely automated.
Confirms 76.5% human-rated and 82.4% software-augmented exposure scores and the caveat that exposure measures time savings, not full automation.

Confirms ~300 LSPs, $8.4B combined revenue +6.6% in 2024, segment growth (+6.3/+8.4/+5.3/-3.7%), roughly flat organic growth with headline growth M&A-driven, and revenue-decline shares 25/33.3/40/41% by tier.

Direct fetch returns 403; verified via reader proxy. Confirms $71.7B 2024 (+5.6%), $75.7B 2025, CAGR cut from 7.0% to 5.0%, $92.3B by 2029, seller's-market and founder-exit commentary, 69.4% increasing MT use, 26.9% vs 13.2% in-house staff cuts, top-three price-pressure challenge, and interpreting-disruption flag. The MTPE penetration figure (26% to 46%, 2022-2024) cited in the a and rho bridges was not found in the retrievable Nimdzi 100 page text; it is corroborated as genuine Nimdzi survey data via Nimdzi's companion MTPE Efficiency Gap publication, so the value stands but the page-level citation is imprecise.

Confirms the WHO/Wordly test (1 of 90 trials passing), NCSC guidance against AI for real-time court interpretation, ABA 'unacceptably unreliable' machine-translation language, and human-review requirements. Advocacy provenance is disclosed by the researcher.
O*NET task list (translate/interpret, terminology research, proofreading, confidentiality ethics) and work-context figures (92% exactness extremely important, 80% daily face-to-face) confirmed.
Confirms 2023 ACS figures: ~68 million speaking a non-English language at home, 29.6 million LEP.

Direct fetch returns 403; verified via reader proxy. Confirms LEP population exceeded 25 million (~9% of population) by 2010 and 80% growth 1990-2010; 2011 publication, 2010 data vintage as disclosed.

The page (fetched twice, including via reader proxy) contains only 'Total US Businesses: 6,841' and none of the cited figures (2,709 businesses, 37,377 employees, ~$1.73B payroll). Used only as qualitative color for e, whose rationale rests on the NAICS definition (S1) and whose n comes from the injected dataset, so the miscitation is not score-driving in effect.
Confirms McKinsey figures: over half of small-business owners 55+, ~1 million of ~6 million transferable businesses expected to sell by 2035, ~$5 trillion value.

caveats: S11 (AverickMedia) does not contain the figures attributed to it (2,709 businesses, 37,377 employees, ~$1.73B payroll); the reachable page shows a different figure (6,841 total US businesses). Because S11 was used only as qualitative color for e — whose 0.85 base is reasoned from the NAICS industry definition (S1) and whose firm count n is dataset-injected — correcting or dropping it would not move F enough to change the CONDITIONAL base tier or the VALIDATE_ASSUMPTIONS action, so this is a citation-hygiene defect, not a material finding.

The MTPE penetration statistic (26% to 46% of firms, 2022-2024) cited to S6 in the a and rho bridges could not be located in the retrievable Nimdzi 100 page text; it is corroborated as genuine Nimdzi survey data through Nimdzi's companion MTPE Efficiency Gap publication. The value is real but should be cited to the specific Nimdzi publication that carries it.

The q bridge relies in part on an uncited pricing claim (human translation commonly $0.15-$0.30/word versus machine translation priced per character/token at a small fraction of that); q's only registered source is S6, which does not carry these per-word figures. q is honestly labeled PROXY with a wide range (0.15-0.45) and its direction-of-error is stated, so this weakens the bridge's documentation rather than its conclusion.

The lens records heterogeneous: false while the narrative's own risk section identifies the commodity-translation versus certified/regulated-interpretation split as a top-three risk and the a, rho, q, and d5 caveats all flag the blend. The default-lens choice is legitimate and disclosed, but the heterogeneity flag understates what the record itself argues; a sub-segment-aware read (which the researcher explicitly recommends for diligence) would likely tighten several ranges.

The researcher's disclosed l-understatement issue (QCEW W-2 wages missing 1099 linguist payments) biases H downward, meaning the record more likely understates than overstates the labor opportunity; this is outside the envelope's interval_scope by design and reaches the reader only through caveats.

s5's 0.38 base rests on buyer-appetite evidence documented at the top-100-LSP scale plus an economy-wide succession base rate; both limitations are disclosed and the 0.22-0.55 range honestly spans them, but the primitive would benefit most from the deal-count evidence the researcher already identifies in change_evidence.
summary: Mechanics pass cleanly (identity, score, and memo all reproduce). All twelve primitive-cited sources were reopened: ten fully support the claims drawn from them, S6 is partially supported (every score-driving claim verified except the MTPE 26-to-46-percent figure, which is corroborated as genuine Nimdzi survey data via a companion Nimdzi publication rather than the cited page), and S11 does not contain the figures attributed to it but was used only as non-score-driving qualitative color for e. Evidence-state discipline is good: nothing researched is labeled OBSERVED, every PROXY names a real source population, transformation, and error direction, and unknowns are handled as disclosed estimates rather than silent zeros. The default lens is used with no deviation; construct boundaries are respected (implementation pace in rho, retention in q, volume in d5/o) with no double counting found; ranges are wide but honest, reflecting a genuinely bimodal industry; and the CONDITIONAL tier, MODEL_CONDITIONED readiness, VALIDATE_ASSUMPTIONS action, and driver/weakness pair all follow directly from the registered evidence. The defects found — one miscited color source, one figure cited to the wrong Nimdzi page, an uncited pricing claim inside the q bridge, and an understated heterogeneity flag — all fail the materiality test individually and collectively, so the record is publishable with caveats.
